title: Abrupt change in the dip of the subducting plate beneath north Chile
abstract: Wide-angle seismic refraction and reflection data from land and sea, complemented by hypocentre data recorded during the 2007 Tocopilla aftershocks, document an abrupt change in the dip of the subducting plate from less than 10° to about 22° at approximately 22°S. The abrupt change occurs at a depth of approximately 20 km at about 60 km landward of the trench. The amphibious seismic experiment includes marine OBS stations and land seismometers. This abrupt dip change is linked to variations in the seismogenic coupling and the structural architecture of the subduction zone at this latitude.

location.description: Offshore north Chile at ~22°S, site of 2007 Tocopilla earthquake, documenting abrupt slab dip change

seismic_lines.name: CINCA95 amphibious profile at ~22°S — 19 OBH + 30 land stations (~420 km total)

data.name: Amphibious wide-angle seismic refraction data (~22°S, Tocopilla)
data.description: Marine OBS + land station wide-angle seismic refraction data at ~22°S offshore Tocopilla. Complemented by 2007 Tocopilla aftershock hypocentres for constraining slab geometry.

data.name: Multibeam bathymetry from SONNE cruises SO104 and SO244 — north Chile

processing.summary: Amphibious wide-angle seismic refraction and reflection data from marine OBS and land seismic stations at ~22°S offshore Tocopilla, combined with 2007 Tocopilla earthquake aftershock hypocentre data. Processing involved travel time tomography to derive the 2D P-wave velocity model and slab geometry. The velocity model combined with hypocentre data documents an abrupt change in slab dip from <10° to ~22° at ~20 km depth, 60 km from the trench.
workflow: 1. Marine OBS deployment and land station installation
3. Phase picking on OBS and land stations
processing.notes: Nature Geoscience letter; behind paywall. Abrupt dip change from <10° to ~22° at ~22°S. Amphibious experiment with marine OBS and onshore seismic stations.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF (Nature Geoscience letter, 4 pages). year_acquired corrected 2007→1995 (CINCA95 cruise, German RV SONNE SO104); 2007 data is only Tocopilla aftershock hypocentres from a local seismological network (not ship-acquired). 19 OBHs + 30 land stations + 2 land shots; profile ~420 km at ~22°S. Acknowledgments: FONDECYT 11090009, German BMBF; data from GeoForschungZentrum Potsdam and IPOC. No source_volume_cui stated in letter (CINCA95 source parameters in Patzwahl et al. 1999). Software: TOMO2D implied.
